🔭 About this Platform

Where Astrophysics Meets the Web

🎓 The Science Behind It

This platform is built by an astrophysics researcher — studying stellar evolution, exoplanet detection via transit photometry and radial velocity, and computational simulations of gravitational dynamics. Every number, every unit, and every habitability metric is grounded in peer-reviewed methods.

🛠 Technology Stack

Astro 4.x
Static site generation + islands
React 18
Interactive UI components
Three.js / R3F
3D WebGL solar system
@react-three/drei
OrbitControls, Stars, Html
Tailwind CSS
Utility-first styling
NASA Exoplanet API
Live planetary data
NASA APOD API
Daily astronomy images
Vercel
Global CDN deployment

📡 Data Sources

  • NASA Exoplanet Archive
    5,700+ confirmed exoplanets
  • NASA APOD API
    Daily astronomy image
  • IAU Minor Planet Center
    Asteroid data
  • Open Exoplanet Catalogue
    Supplemental data

⚙️ Habitability Index

The habitability score shown on each exoplanet card is a simplified proxy for the Earth Similarity Index (ESI). It weighs:

  • 🔵 Radius (60%) — planets near Earth's size (0.7–1.5 R⊕) score highest
  • 🟡 Orbital period (40%) — periods 200–600 days suggest habitable-zone positioning

Note: True habitability requires stellar luminosity, eccentricity, atmospheric data, and more. This is a first-order approximation from available catalog data.
